paper_title: Machine learning models for forecasting employee demand in healthcare HR
authors: ST Hasan
journal_name: Journal of Engineering and Computational Intelligence
abstract: The discussion section notes that the results provide empirical evidence of the increasing role of machine learning in forecasting healthcare workforce demand, highlighting how such models can support more accurate human resource planning in healthcare systems.

paper_title: Machine learning-driven internet of medical things (ML-IoMT)-based healthcare monitoring system
authors: KSL Kazi
journal_name: Responsible AI for digital health and medical analytics
abstract: The work utilises seven machine learning classification algorithms to develop an ML-IoMT-based healthcare monitoring system targeting conditions including heart, respiratory and kidney disorders, achieving a reported minimum accuracy of 82.3% and a maximum accuracy above that threshold.

paper_title: Harnessing AI and machine learning for precision medicine: Advancements in genomic research, disease detection, and personalized healthcare
authors: M Recharla, C Chakilam, S Kannan
abstract: The article reviews machine learning-based disease diagnosis and discusses how machine learning in healthcare is gaining global prominence by improving precision medicine, genomic research, early disease detection and personalised treatment strategies compared with traditional diagnostic approaches.

paper_title: Precision healthcare for UTIs: leveraging machine learning to reduce readmissions
authors: OM Al-Jbour, M Alshraideh
abstract: The study proposes a method employing machine learning to automatically extract and analyse risk factors related to urinary tract infections, aiming to reduce hospital readmissions and the associated financial burden on healthcare systems through precision healthcare interventions.

paper_title: Machine learning enables legal risk assessment in internet healthcare using HIPAA data
authors: S Liu, H Liu, S Fan, L Song, Z Wang
abstract: This study investigates how artificial intelligence and machine learning analytical models applied to HIPAA-related data can enhance regulatory capacity by supporting legal risk assessment in internet healthcare, improving oversight of compliance and potential violations.

paper_title: Analysis of geospatial variations in healthcare across rural communities in the US using machine learning
authors: R Svynarenko, H Kim, T Stansberry, C Oh, A Sarkar
abstract: The paper uses the Automatic machine learning (AutoML) package within H2O to model geospatial variations in healthcare characteristics across rural US communities, automating training and tuning of multiple machine learning models to uncover spatial disparities in healthcare access and outcomes.

paper_title: Machine learning in predictive modelling: Addressing chronic disease management through optimized healthcare processes
authors: A Olalekan Kehinde
abstract: The article argues that collaborative efforts among healthcare providers and data scientists, enabled by machine learning-based predictive modelling, can transform chronic disease management by optimising healthcare processes and improving clinical decision-making.

paper_title: Combining machine learning and dynamic system techniques to early detection of respiratory outbreaks in routinely collected primary healthcare records
authors: DGF Borges, ER Coutinho, T Cerqueira-Silva
abstract: The research combines unsupervised machine learning methods with dynamical system techniques to detect early signs of respiratory outbreaks based on routinely collected primary healthcare encounter data, identifying increases in encounters that may signal emerging outbreaks.

paper_title: Data Analysis and Information Processing Frameworks for Ethical Artificial Intelligence Implementation: Machine-Learning Algorithm Validation in Clinical Research …
authors: J Rane, RA Chaudhari, NL Rane
abstract: The work discusses data analysis and information-processing frameworks for validating machine learning algorithms in clinical research, drawing lessons for clinicians, technology developers and policymakers who aim to deploy machine learning in healthcare practice while addressing ethical and regulatory considerations.

paper_title: Leveraging Machine Learning for Enhanced Patient Engagement and Outcomes: Revolutionizing Healthcare Marketing
authors: R Swadhi, K Gayathri, NV Suresh
abstract: This chapter examines how integrating machine learning into healthcare marketing can enhance patient engagement and outcomes, while also addressing challenges such as data privacy, algorithmic bias and ethical concerns in the deployment of machine learning in healthcare.

paper_title: Revolutionizing patient care: the synergy of IoT and machine learning in smart healthcare
authors: HA Sanghvi, D Gangwani, AA Mohamed, P Gajjar
abstract: The article discusses how integrating Internet of Things (IoT) technologies with machine learning in smart healthcare can enhance operations for medical practitioners, institutions, patients, and the pharmaceutical sector, emphasizing the role of machine learning in improving healthcare services.

paper_title: Enhancing obesity detection diagnostics: The synergy of machine learning and AI
authors: T Matharasi, A Jaggi, R Patneedi
abstract: This study evaluates and compares multiple machine learning approaches for obesity detection diagnostics to support efficient and effective healthcare planning, highlighting the potential of advanced machine learning techniques in obesity-related healthcare applications.

paper_title: Comparison of methods for tuning machine learning model hyper-parameters: with application to predicting high-need high-cost health care users
authors: C Meaney, X Wang, J Guan, TA Stukel
abstract: The study compares methods for tuning hyper-parameters of a supervised machine learning model—specifically an extreme gradient boosting model—designed to predict binary high-need high-cost health care user status, focusing on model performance and optimization.

paper_title: Comprehensive feature selection for machine learning-based intrusion detection in healthcare IoMT networks
authors: M Rehman, R Kalakoti, H Bahşi
abstract: The paper addresses increasing vulnerability of Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) healthcare networks to cyberattacks and investigates comprehensive feature selection strategies to improve machine learning-based intrusion detection performance in such environments.

paper_title: Development and validation of a cost-effective machine learning model for screening potential rheumatoid arthritis in primary healthcare clinics
authors: W Wu, X Hu, L Yan, Z Li, B Li, X Chen
abstract: Using ten classical machine learning algorithms, this study develops a cost-effective model to screen potential rheumatoid arthritis cases in primary healthcare clinics, then retrains and validates the model on data from two primary healthcare settings.

paper_title: Optimizing healthcare management systems with AI and machine learning
authors: R Gupta, N Gusain, BS Shirole, MT Jagtap
abstract: The work explores optimization of healthcare management systems using artificial intelligence and machine learning, including discussion of concepts such as Causal Economic Machine Learning in health care and their implications for healthcare operations.

paper_title: Machine learning approaches for predicting and diagnosing chronic kidney disease: current trends, challenges, solutions, and future directions
authors: P Gogoi, JA Valan
abstract: The article reviews machine learning approaches for predicting and diagnosing chronic kidney disease, discussing data diversity challenges, limitations from small and less diverse datasets, and their impact on generalizability of healthcare models, while outlining potential solutions and future directions.

paper_title: Healthcare Insurance Fraud Detection Using Machine Learning
authors: C Chitteti, M Yamuna, M Srinath
abstract: The paper addresses healthcare insurance fraud as a major source of financial loss and erosion of trust, proposing machine learning-based methods to detect fraudulent healthcare insurance claims and improve fraud management.

paper_title: A comparative study of machine learning algorithms for fall detection in technology-based healthcare system: Analyzing SVM, KNN, decision tree, random forest …
authors: L Afuan, RR Isnanto
abstract: This study compares six machine learning algorithms, including SVM, KNN, decision tree, and random forest, for fall detection in technology-based healthcare systems, with emphasis on elderly care and evaluation of algorithm performance.

paper_title: A meta-analysis of AI and machine learning in project management: Optimizing vaccine development for emerging viral threats in biotechnology
authors: J Vaghasiya, M Khan, TM Bakhda
abstract: The meta-analysis examines applications of artificial intelligence and machine learning in project management for optimizing vaccine development against emerging viral threats, with subgroup analyses across healthcare sectors and biotechnology domains.
